protocols.Paper_title: Analysis of Inactivation of SARS-CoV-2 by Specimen Transport Media, Nucleic Acid Extraction Reagents, Detergents, and Fixatives
protocols.Evidence: treated sample was purified through filtration matrices; eluate was used directly in cell culture

protocols.Paper_title: Analysis of Inactivation of SARS-CoV-2 by Specimen Transport Media, Nucleic Acid Extraction Reagents, Detergents, and Fixatives
protocols.Evidence: Specimen transport tubes are designed to inactivate microorganisms while preserving nucleic acids for molecular testing

protocols.Paper_title: Analysis of Inactivation of SARS-CoV-2 by Specimen Transport Media, Nucleic Acid Extraction Reagents, Detergents, and Fixatives
protocols.Evidence: molecular extraction lysis buffers used in several manual and automated extraction protocols

protocols.Paper_title: Analysis of Inactivation of SARS-CoV-2 by Specimen Transport Media, Nucleic Acid Extraction Reagents, Detergents, and Fixatives
protocols.Evidence: evaluated Triton X-100, SDS, NP-40, and Tween 20; extracted RNA from treated samples was tested using qRT-PCR

protocols.Paper_title: Analysis of Inactivation of SARS-CoV-2 by Specimen Transport Media, Nucleic Acid Extraction Reagents, Detergents, and Fixatives
protocols.Evidence: formaldehyde or formaldehyde and glutaraldehyde is a well-established protocol, particularly for diagnostic electron microscopy

protocols.Paper_title: Analysis of Inactivation of SARS-CoV-2 by Specimen Transport Media, Nucleic Acid Extraction Reagents, Detergents, and Fixatives
protocols.Evidence: PHMB is a polymer used as a disinfectant and antiseptic, evaluated here as a potential lysis buffer
